tools.name: group-items_delete
tools.description: Removes an item from a group. Optional: id. Returns the EmptyResponse. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: The `id` parameter specifies the YouTube group item ID of the group item that is being deleted.

tools.name: group-items_insert
tools.description: Creates a group item. Optional: errors, etag, groupId (plus 3 more). Returns the new GroupItem.

params.name: errors
params.description: Request Error information. The presence of an error field signals that the operation has failed.

params.name: etag
params.description: The Etag of this resource.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The ID that YouTube uses to uniquely identify the group that contains the item.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID that YouTube uses to uniquely identify the `channel`, `video`, `playlist`, or `asset` resource that is included in the group. Note that this ID refers specifically to the inclusion of that resource in a particular group and is different than the channel ID, video ID, playlist ID, or asset ID that uniquely identifies the resource itself. The `resource.id` property's value specifies the unique channel, video, playlist, or asset ID.

params.name: kind
params.description: Identifies the API resource's type. The value will be `youtube#groupItem`.

params.name: resource
params.description: Resource

tools.name: group-items_list
tools.description: Returns a collection of group items that match the API request parameters. Optional: groupId.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The `groupId` parameter specifies the unique ID of the group for which you want to retrieve group items.

tools.name: groups_delete
tools.description: Deletes a group. Optional: id. Returns the EmptyResponse. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: The `id` parameter specifies the YouTube group ID of the group that is being deleted.

tools.name: groups_insert
tools.description: Creates a group. Optional: contentDetails, errors, etag (plus 3 more). Returns the new Group.

params.name: contentDetails
params.description: A group's content details.

params.name: errors
params.description: Request Error information. The presence of an error field signals that the operation has failed.

params.name: etag
params.description: The Etag of this resource.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID that YouTube uses to uniquely identify the group.

params.name: kind
params.description: Identifies the API resource's type. The value will be `youtube#group`.

params.name: snippet
params.description: A group snippet.

tools.name: groups_list
tools.description: Returns a collection of groups that match the API request parameters. For example, you can retrieve all groups that the authenticated user owns, or you can retrieve one or more groups by their unique IDs. Optional: id, mine, pageToken.

params.name: id
params.description: The `id` parameter specifies a comma-separated list of the YouTube group ID(s) for the resource(s) that are being retrieved. Each group must be owned by the authenticated user. In a `group` resource, the `id` property specifies the group's YouTube group ID. Note that if you do not specify a value for the `id` parameter, then you must set the `mine` parameter to `true`.

params.name: mine
params.description: This parameter can only be used in a properly authorized request. Set this parameter's value to true to retrieve all groups owned by the authenticated user.

params.name: pageToken
params.description: The `pageToken` parameter identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. In an API response, the `nextPageToken` property identifies the next page that can be retrieved.

tools.name: groups_update
tools.description: Modifies a group. For example, you could change a group's title. Optional: contentDetails, errors, etag (plus 3 more). Returns the updated Group.

params.name: contentDetails
params.description: A group's content details.

params.name: errors
params.description: Request Error information. The presence of an error field signals that the operation has failed.

params.name: etag
params.description: The Etag of this resource.

params.name: id
params.description: The ID that YouTube uses to uniquely identify the group.

params.name: kind
params.description: Identifies the API resource's type. The value will be `youtube#group`.

params.name: snippet
params.description: A group snippet.

tools.name: reports_query
tools.description: Retrieve your YouTube Analytics reports. Optional: currency, dimensions, endDate (plus 8 more). Returns the QueryResponse.

params.name: currency
params.description: The currency to which financial metrics should be converted. The default is US Dollar (USD). If the result contains no financial metrics, this flag will be ignored. Responds with an error if the specified currency is not recognized.', pattern: [A-Z]{3}

params.name: dimensions
params.description: A comma-separated list of YouTube Analytics dimensions, such as `views` or `ageGroup,gender`. See the [Available Reports](/youtube/analytics/v2/available_reports) document for a list of the reports that you can retrieve and the dimensions used for those reports. Also see the [Dimensions](/youtube/analytics/v2/dimsmets/dims) document for definitions of those dimensions.' pattern: [0-9a-zA-Z,]+

params.name: endDate
params.description: The end date for fetching YouTube Analytics data. The value should be in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. required: true, pattern: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}

params.name: filters
params.description: A list of filters that should be applied when retrieving YouTube Analytics data. The [Available Reports](/youtube/analytics/v2/available_reports) document identifies the dimensions that can be used to filter each report, and the [Dimensions](/youtube/analytics/v2/dimsmets/dims) document defines those dimensions. If a request uses multiple filters, join them together with a semicolon (`;`), and the returned result table will satisfy both filters. For example, a filters parameter value of `video==dMH0bHeiRNg;country==IT` restricts the result set to include data for the given video in Italy.',

params.name: ids
params.description: Identifies the YouTube channel or content owner for which you are retrieving YouTube Analytics data. - To request data for a YouTube user, set the `ids` parameter value to `channel==CHANNEL_ID`, where `CHANNEL_ID` specifies the unique YouTube channel ID. - To request data for a YouTube CMS content owner, set the `ids` parameter value to `contentOwner==OWNER_NAME`, where `OWNER_NAME` is the CMS name of the content owner. required: true, pattern: [a-zA-Z]+==[a-zA-Z0-9_+-]+

params.name: includeHistoricalChannelData
params.description: If set to true historical data (i.e. channel data from before the linking of the channel to the content owner) will be retrieved.',

params.name: maxResults
params.description: The maximum number of rows to include in the response.', minValue: 1

params.name: metrics
params.description: A comma-separated list of YouTube Analytics metrics, such as `views` or `likes,dislikes`. See the [Available Reports](/youtube/analytics/v2/available_reports) document for a list of the reports that you can retrieve and the metrics available in each report, and see the [Metrics](/youtube/analytics/v2/dimsmets/mets) document for definitions of those metrics. required: true, pattern: [0-9a-zA-Z,]+

params.name: sort
params.description: A comma-separated list of dimensions or metrics that determine the sort order for YouTube Analytics data. By default the sort order is ascending. The '`-`' prefix causes descending sort order.', pattern: [-0-9a-zA-Z,]+

params.name: startDate
params.description: The start date for fetching YouTube Analytics data. The value should be in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. required: true, pattern: '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}

params.name: startIndex
params.description: An index of the first entity to retrieve. Use this parameter as a pagination mechanism along with the max-results parameter (one-based, inclusive).', minValue: 1
